source_categories.manual: Owner manual or operation-guide references for user-visible behavior.
source_categories.schematic: Service schematic or service-photo references for hardware signal context.
source_categories.firmware: User-supplied MPC2000XL OS image metadata or runtime traces kept outside git.
source_categories.vmpc: VMPC docs, source, and license review references for comparative research.
source_categories.jjos: JJ-OS investigation boundary; not accepted as MPC2000XL evidence until verified.
source_categories.internal_spec: Repo-owned product, conformance, spike, and implementation specs.

behaviors.name: Core starts on MAIN screen

source_coverage.note: Owner-manual page or section for startup screen is not mapped yet.
No service schematic signal mapping is needed for this UI seed yet.
No user-supplied firmware trace has been captured for initial boot state.
VMPC comparative behavior remains pending docs/source/license review.
JJ-OS for MPC2000XL is unverified and must not be used as acceptance evidence.
Seed behavior is currently backed by repo-owned product and conformance specs.

conflict_notes: Manual, firmware, VMPC, and hardware evidence are not mapped yet; this remains an internal-spec seed.

behaviors.name: Default program resolves assigned pads to synthetic sample metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for PROGRAM sample assignment behavior is pending.
No firmware trace has been captured for program assignment defaults.
The original foundation introduced generated synthetic metadata assignments.
Default assignment coverage is now A01-D16.

outputs: PadTriggered
SamplePlaybackIntent for assigned pads
SamplePlaybackMiss for unassigned pads

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec/manual-investigation foundation and does not include proprietary samples or copied program content.

behaviors.name: Sample playback intent renders deterministic synthetic stereo PCM metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual mapping for audio playback, pan law, voice behavior, and timing is pending.
No firmware trace has been captured for sample playback audio output or timing.
Renderer is deterministic rights-safe generated synthesis and reports zero loaded audio bytes.

conflict_notes: This is internal-spec/manual-investigation audio scaffolding pending exact source mapping; it is not a host audio integration and includes no proprietary audio bytes.

behaviors.name: PROGRAM soft keys clear and restore selected pad assignment

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for PROGRAM soft-key assignment editing is pending.
F1 Clear and F2 Assign are deterministic internal controls for this foundation slice.

state.selected_program_pad: updated by PROGRAM pad strikes or cursor/data wheel

outputs: PadAssignmentChanged
LcdChanged

conflict_notes: Soft-key labels and exact edit flow are internal-spec pending manual evidence; no proprietary screen art or copied text is stored.

behaviors.name: Recorded pad events snapshot synthetic sample assignment metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact reference behavior for recorded event/program assignment coupling is pending.
Recorded events store an optional playback intent snapshot when a pad has an assignment.

state.playback: optional SamplePlaybackIntent snapshot for assigned pads

outputs: PadTriggered
SamplePlaybackIntent
SequenceEventRecorded

conflict_notes: This preserves deterministic assignment metadata for future playback scheduling but is not a claim of exact MPC2000XL event storage format.

behaviors.name: MAIN cursor left/right changes selected edit field

behaviors.name: MAIN data wheel edits selected field

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for sequence, track, tempo, and bar edits is pending.
Current ranges are deterministic internal-spec ranges until reference evidence is mapped.

outputs: LcdChanged

conflict_notes: Tempo clamp reuses the foundation range; sequence, track, and bars are bounded internal behavior until exact reference evidence is mapped.

behaviors.name: MAIN Track+ and Track- soft keys change selected track

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for MAIN Track+ and Track- soft keys is pending.
Only soft keys 2 and 3 are implemented in this slice; other soft keys return structured Ignored output.

outputs: LcdChanged
Ignored for unimplemented soft keys

behaviors.name: Sequence recording stores pad strikes while playing and recording

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for sequence recording and overdub behavior is pending.
No user-supplied firmware trace has been captured for sequence event storage yet.
Current behavior is an internal-spec/manual-investigation foundation toward full app behavior, not a reference-accuracy claim.

state.playing_and_recording: pad strike appends recorded sequence event
state.stopped_or_armed_only: pad strike does not append recorded sequence event

outputs: PadTriggered
SamplePlaybackIntent or SamplePlaybackMiss
SequenceEventRecorded when playing and recording

conflict_notes: Manual page mapping is incomplete; no proprietary sequence screens or copied manual content are stored.

behaviors.name: Transport ticks advance a deterministic internal playhead while playing

source_coverage.note: Exact timing resolution and manual page mapping for playhead tick behavior are pending.
No firmware trace has been captured to validate PPQN or tick rounding behavior.
The current 96 PPQN playhead is an internal deterministic foundation pending exact source mapping.

state.overflow: large micros values are handled without panics or wrapping

outputs: LcdChanged when visible playhead text changes

conflict_notes: PPQN and exact timing display are internal-spec pending manual, firmware, or hardware evidence.

behaviors.name: Recorded sequence events schedule stored playback intents while playing

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual mapping for sequence playback timing and event replay behavior is pending.
No firmware or hardware trace has validated event crossing windows, PPQN, or latency behavior.
Current scheduling is deterministic foundation behavior, not final MPC2000XL reference accuracy.

state.playing: events schedule when previous_playhead_ticks < event.tick <= new_playhead_ticks
state.last_playback: last scheduled SamplePlaybackIntent in the tick batch

outputs: SequenceEventPlayed for scheduled events with playback metadata
SamplePlaybackIntent using stored recorded metadata
LcdChanged when visible playhead text changes

desktop.host_audio: every SamplePlaybackIntent in the output batch is routed in order

conflict_notes: Looping now has a bounded internal-spec foundation in core.sequence.loop-locate; exact bar/beat/tick display, timing correction, multi-cycle loop catch-up, and latency remain unmapped pending manual, firmware, or hardware evidence.

behaviors.name: Sequence locate start and bounded loop wrap playback

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual mapping for LOCATE, sequence loop state, and end-of-sequence transport policy is pending.
No firmware or hardware trace has validated loop boundary scheduling, tick-zero replay, or stopped-at-end behavior.
JJ-OS for MPC2000XL remains unverified and is not used as acceptance evidence for this slice.
Current locate and loop behavior is deterministic foundation behavior, not final MPC2000XL reference accuracy.

state.locate_start: playhead_ticks and playhead_tick_remainder reset to 0 without changing play/record flags
state.loop_disabled: crossing sequence end clamps to sequence length and stops transport
state.loop_enabled: crossing sequence end wraps to start and keeps transport playing
state.tick_zero: recorded tick 0 events schedule only at loop boundary, not ordinary playback from tick 0
state.persistence: loop_enabled round-trips in sequence snapshots and defaults false when missing

outputs: PlayheadLocated for locate start
LoopChanged for loop toggle
TransportChanged when non-loop playback stops at sequence end
SequenceEventPlayed and SamplePlaybackIntent for stored playback metadata across loop boundary

desktop.controls: LOCATE START and LOOP toggle near transport
desktop.status: loop state, sequence length, and playhead ticks visible

conflict_notes: This is internal-spec 4/4 loop behavior. Exact MPC2000XL or JJ-OS loop policy, multi-cycle host tick catch-up, bar/beat/tick display, timing correction, and overdub merge semantics remain unmapped.

behaviors.name: MAIN track mute skips muted-track events during sequence playback

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual mapping for MAIN track mute controls, dedicated Track Mute mode, and playback side effects is pending.
No firmware or hardware trace has validated track mute persistence, scheduler filtering, or audio-engine behavior.
JJ-OS track mute behavior is not used as acceptance evidence for this internal foundation slice.
Current track mute behavior is deterministic foundation behavior, not final MPC2000XL or JJ-OS parity.

state.persistence: muted_tracks round-trips in sequence snapshots and defaults empty when missing
state.playback: recorded events on muted tracks are skipped
state.recording: muted selected tracks still record metadata
state.erase: MAIN F5 still erases selected-track events while muted

outputs: TrackMuteChanged for MAIN soft key 4
LcdChanged after successful mute toggle
No SequenceEventPlayed or SamplePlaybackIntent for skipped muted-track events

desktop.status: selected-track mute state and muted track count visible

conflict_notes: This is an internal MAIN playback-gating foundation only. Dedicated Track Mute mode, solo interaction, JJ-OS extensions, mute automation, MIDI output muting, voice stealing, and audio tail cutoff remain unmapped.

behaviors.name: MIDI note-on maps the default 36..=51 range to bank A pads

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual page mapping for MIDI input channel, pad note mapping, and note-off behavior is pending.
No user-supplied firmware or hardware MIDI trace has validated note mapping, velocity handling, or note-off behavior.
No MIDI input electrical or board-level evidence is mapped to this deterministic event simulation.
Current MIDI input is a typed internal event contract without host device I/O.

inputs.note: default 36..=51; configurable by MIDI settings base note

mapping.notes_36_to_51: default bank A pads 1..=16 using pad = note - 35; MIDI settings can move this 16-note window
mapping.note_off: no playback and no sequence recording

outputs: MidiNoteMapped for mapped note-on
PadTriggered through the existing pad path
SamplePlaybackIntent or SamplePlaybackMiss through the existing pad path
SequenceEventRecorded when playing and recording

desktop.simulation_controls: channel, note, velocity, note-on, note-off, and quick note buttons
desktop.host_audio: mapped SamplePlaybackIntent outputs continue through host audio render handling

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec/manual-investigation MIDI foundation and does not include host MIDI I/O, firmware behavior claims, JJ-OS behavior, hardware captures, or proprietary MIDI/audio assets.

behaviors.name: MIDI mode edits simulated note input channel and base note

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL MIDI settings screen copy, channel policy, and base-note/pad mapping policy are pending.
No user-supplied firmware or hardware MIDI trace has validated settings edit behavior or channel filtering.
JJ-OS parity is not claimed for this MIDI settings foundation.
Current MIDI settings affect simulated note input and internal MIDI output base-note policy; native host MIDI device I/O remains absent.

inputs.policy: input channel filter then base-note range mapping

state.mapped_note_range: base_note..=base_note+15 maps to bank A pads 1..=16
state.snapshot: machine-level MIDI settings persist with current-reader serde defaults for missing fields; older-binary forward compatibility for new v1 fields is deferred to the formal snapshot migration/versioning slice

lcd.midi: input channel, base note, mapped note range, selected settings field, and Host MIDI Out capture boundary

outputs: MidiSettingsChanged plus LcdChanged for MIDI settings edits
MidiInputIgnored for valid note-on events blocked by selected input channel
MidiNoteMapped for valid note-on events accepted by Omni or matching selected input channel

desktop.status: input channel, base note, mapped note range, selected field, and host output capture boundary
desktop.controls: MIDI-mode setting/value buttons route through cursor/data-wheel events near MIDI sim

conflict_notes: This is internal-spec MIDI settings behavior for simulated note input and deterministic output capture only.
Native host MIDI device I/O, MIDI clock, MIDI sync, MIDI thru, raw MIDI event persistence, exact MPC2000XL screen parity, and JJ-OS parity remain unmapped.

behaviors.name: Successful pad and sequence playback emit deterministic MIDI output intents for host capture

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL MIDI output channel, note, thru, note-off, and sync policies are pending.
No user-supplied firmware or hardware MIDI output trace has validated this internal output mapping.
MIDI connector and output electrical behavior are not yet mapped to native host device I/O.
JJ-OS parity is not claimed for this MIDI output capture foundation.
Current behavior is typed output intent plus capture backend only; no OS MIDI ports are opened.

inputs.policy: successful SamplePlaybackIntent emits MidiOutputIntent
successful unmuted scheduled SamplePlaybackIntent emits MidiOutputIntent
mapped incoming MIDI note-on triggers playback without echoing MidiOutputIntent

intent.fields: track, program, pad bank/pad, source sample id/name, channel, note, velocity
intent.channel_policy: selected track maps to channel 1..=16 by wrapping
intent.note_policy: current MIDI base note plus A-D 64-pad linear index when the note remains in 0..=127

host_capture.backend: mpc_midi CaptureMidiBackend retains bounded recent note messages

desktop.routing: MachineOutput::MidiOutputIntent routes to enabled capture backend
desktop.status: queued, ignored, failed counts and last host MIDI event visible

conflict_notes: This is a deterministic internal output/capture contract, not native host MIDI device I/O.
MIDI clock, MIDI sync, MIDI thru, note-off output, raw MIDI event persistence, device discovery, exact MPC2000XL screen parity, and JJ-OS parity remain unmapped.

behaviors.name: Disabled host audio ignores playback without backend enqueue

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL host/device audio behavior and UI enable semantics are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for host output enable/disable behavior.
No DAC, output jack, or device routing evidence is mapped for this plumbing slice.
Disabled behavior is a repo-owned safety contract for the full app while exact device evidence is pending.

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec/manual-investigation safety behavior and does not claim exact MPC2000XL audio output behavior.

behaviors.name: Enabled host audio enqueues deterministic rendered audio through capture backend

source_coverage.note: Owner-manual mapping for exact audio output behavior remains pending.
No firmware trace validates voice scheduling, DAC output, latency, or device routing yet.
No hardware output path evidence is mapped to this deterministic capture backend.
Capture and null backends are deterministic test plumbing fed by the rights-safe renderer.

backend.stored_data: render summaries and frame counts only

conflict_notes: This slice is internal-spec/manual-investigation plumbing toward the full app; exact audio/device evidence remains pending.

behaviors.name: Count-in metronome click intents render deterministic PCM and route through host audio

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL metronome click timbre, output level, and routing behavior are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace validates count-in click audio rendering, latency, or scheduling tolerance yet.
No hardware output path evidence is mapped to this deterministic generated click.
The click audio contract is deterministic generated PCM from CountInClickIntent and AudioRenderSettings.

render.frames: deterministic stereo PCM i16 bounded by AudioRenderSettings.frame_count
render.accent: accent peak amplitude is greater than non-accent peak amplitude

host_audio_state.disabled: ignored without backend enqueue or success-counter increments
host_audio_state.enabled: enqueues through the existing play_rendered path and increments accepted counters

desktop.status: continues to show count-in bar, beat, and tick position
desktop.host_audio_status: last event and counters reflect click enqueues when host audio is enabled

conflict_notes: This is an internal deterministic audio-routing foundation, not a real MPC2000XL metronome sound or real device backend.
No proprietary samples, recordings, firmware bytes, external media, copied manuals, service scans, or binary audio fixtures are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: Host audio tracks deterministic active voices and stealing

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL voice lifecycle, polyphony limit, stealing, envelopes, mute groups, and note-off behavior are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace validates host voice ids, stealing order, completion timing, or scheduler tolerance yet.
No hardware output path or DAC evidence is mapped to this deterministic host accounting slice.
JJ-OS voice behavior is not accepted as MPC2000XL evidence for this foundation slice.
The 32-voice default, clamped configurable range, active-voice summaries, and oldest-voice stealing are internal foundation policy only.

inputs.policy: allocates one deterministic active voice only after render validation, backend success, receipt validation, queued receipt, and positive frame count
zero is a no-op; positive values reduce all active remaining frame counts and remove completed voices
default 32; configurable constructor clamps to 1..=128

voice_allocation.stolen_voice_id: reported when oldest active voice was stolen before allocation

active_voice_summary.source_label: render summary label only; no PCM exposure
active_voice_summary.remaining_frame_count: queued render frame count at allocation

voice_advance.remaining_frame_count: saturating subtraction by requested frame count
voice_advance.completed_voice_count: increments for voices removed after remaining frames reach zero

voice_stealing.policy: oldest active voice is removed before allocating the new voice when active count reaches limit
voice_stealing.stolen_voice_count: increments only when an active voice is stolen
voice_stealing.receipt: voice allocation receipt reports allocated voice id and stolen voice id when present

no_allocation: backend receipts that are not queued

desktop.host_audio_status: compact mode/backend/render counter plus voice count summary
desktop.sample_event_text: existing sample playback enqueue and failure wording is preserved

conflict_notes: This is internal deterministic host accounting, not accepted evidence of exact MPC2000XL polyphony, stealing, envelopes, mute groups, note-off behavior, DAC output, or timing.
No real audio device backend, proprietary samples, recordings, firmware bytes, external media, copied manuals, service scans, or binary audio fixtures are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: Project snapshot round-trips sequence, program, and synthetic metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL project save/load workflow, storage naming, and file format evidence are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace or binary format evidence has been captured for native project persistence.
VMPC comparative project persistence behavior remains pending docs/source/license review.
The current JSON snapshot is repo-owned metadata-only persistence toward the full app, not a claim about native MPC2000XL disk formats.

state.sequence: index, name, tempo, selected track, bars, playhead ticks, and recorded events restore
state.program: current program index/name, selected program pad, and pad assignments restore
state.synthetic_metadata: sample ids/names in assignments and recorded playback metadata restore

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec/manual-investigation JSON snapshot foundation toward the full app; exact MPC2000XL storage and file-format evidence remains pending.

behaviors.name: Project file save/load stores repo-owned metadata-only JSON snapshots

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL project file naming, disk save/load workflow, and invalid-file UX are not mapped yet.
No user-supplied firmware trace or native binary project/disk format evidence has been captured for file persistence.
VMPC comparative project file behavior remains pending docs/source/license review.
The file format is repo-owned JSON metadata produced by mpc_core snapshots, not a native MPC2000XL disk-format claim.

snapshot.validation: load delegates project JSON validation to mpc_core

errors: wrong suffixes are rejected before writing
malformed or invalid project JSON returns structured storage errors

conflict_notes: This is a rights-safe host JSON storage foundation; native MPC2000XL disk structures, project file compatibility, and proprietary binary formats remain evidence gaps.

behaviors.name: DISK mode requests host project JSON save/load operations

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL DISK screen flow, file naming, disk navigation, and error UX are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace or native disk image behavior is used for this request-only foundation.
DISK mode emits requests for existing repo-owned project JSON storage and does not parse or write Akai proprietary disk formats.

inputs.policy: non-zero delta toggles selected operation by sign; zero delta is ignored

state.selected_disk_operation: defaults to save_project, persists in machine snapshots, and missing-field restore defaults to save_project
state.snapshot: PROJECT_SNAPSHOT_VERSION remains 1; current-reader missing-field restore is supported; foundation builds explicitly allow additive v1 JSON fields until formal migration/versioning defines a stable older-binary compatibility promise

outputs: DiskOperationSelected plus LcdChanged when selected_disk_operation changes
DiskOperationRequested save_project for DISK F2
DiskOperationRequested load_project for DISK F3
Ignored for DISK F5/F6 in this slice
Non-DISK soft keys do not emit disk operation requests

lcd.title: DISK
lcd.body: selected operation marker, project-file JSON boundary, virtual host-path disk wording, and no-native-format notice

desktop.status: existing project file path/status controls remain the visible storage surface

conflict_notes: This is not native MPC2000XL disk-format support and does not parse or write Akai proprietary formats.
No manuals, scans, firmware, images, samples, audio bytes, third-party code/assets, or binary disk fixtures are added.
DISK F5/F6 are intentionally unmapped to avoid implying a broader disk workflow before evidence exists.
MAIN erase and PROGRAM soft-key behavior remain isolated from DISK project-operation requests.

behaviors.name: Project snapshot restore validates version, ranges, duplicate pads, and transport safety

source_coverage.note: Manual evidence for exact project-load transport behavior and invalid-file UX is not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for invalid project data handling.
Restore validation is a repo-owned safety contract for metadata-only snapshots.

invalid_data: out-of-range track, pad, velocity, tempo, and bar values are rejected
duplicate pad assignment collisions are rejected

transport.note: transport is intentionally not persisted in this slice

lcd.refresh: LCD is rebuilt from restored state

conflict_notes: Transport safety and validation behavior are internal-spec/manual-investigation guardrails pending exact MPC2000XL project-load evidence.

behaviors.name: PROGRAM pad Level Pan Tune edits propagate to playback and render metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact owner-manual PROGRAM parameter field flow, text, and ranges are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for PROGRAM Level, Pan, or Tune edit behavior.
VMPC comparative PROGRAM parameter behavior remains pending docs/source/license review.
Level, Pan, and Tune editing is currently an internal foundation contract with explicit reference-behavior gaps.

outputs: PadParameterChanged for assigned Level/Pan/Tune edits
Ignored for unassigned Level/Pan/Tune edits
SamplePlaybackIntent carries level, pan, and tune_cents

recording.snapshot: recorded SequenceEvent playback metadata preserves level, pan, and tune_cents after later assignment edits

behaviors.name: A-D pad bank controls select the active 64-pad program surface

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL and JJ-OS pad-bank UI policies are pending reference verification.
Current behavior is a deterministic foundation and not a reference-accuracy claim.

inputs.note: default 36..=51; configurable by MIDI settings base note
inputs.limitation: always maps to bank A until active MIDI bank policy is verified

state.pad_bank: front-panel bank controls and explicit pad strikes update active bank
state.selected_program_pad: bank controls preserve pad number while changing bank; PROGRAM strikes select struck bank/pad
state.default_program_assignments: Program01 generates A01-D16 synthetic metadata assignments
state.project_restore: snapshots with fewer than 64 assignments remain valid; missing pads resolve as unassigned

outputs: BankChanged plus LcdChanged for bank controls
SamplePlaybackIntent or SamplePlaybackMiss uses the actual bank
SequenceEventRecorded stores bank-specific playback metadata

desktop.status: active bank shown alongside selected program pad

conflict_notes: MIDI note-on intentionally remains bank-A-only until a verified active MIDI bank policy is mapped.
No proprietary pad-bank manual text, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, factory samples, or hardware scans are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: MAIN F5 erase removes the latest selected-track sequence event

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL erase screen flow, filters, ranges, confirmation, and undo behavior are not mapped yet.
JJ-OS erase behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this slice.
The current contract is erase-latest-current-track foundation behavior.

success: SequenceEventsErased with selected_track, count, and erased event metadata
LcdChanged with reduced MAIN recorded-event count

state.scope: only the latest event on selected_track is removed
state.order: remaining events preserve their original order
state.playhead: playhead ticks and fractional remainder are preserved
state.last_playback: preserved because erase is sequence metadata editing

playback.scheduling: erased events are not scheduled after erase

conflict_notes: This is not a full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS erase mode clone; range, filter, all-track, confirmation, and undo semantics remain deferred.
No proprietary manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, factory samples, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: SAMPLE and TRIM navigate a metadata-only generated sample catalog

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL sample record, sample list, and trim editing behavior is not mapped yet.
JJ-OS SAMPLE/TRIM behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this slice.
The current contract is rights-safe metadata catalog navigation.

inputs.policy: delta clamps to catalog bounds

state.catalog: derived from current-program sample metadata, sorted by source pad and deduped by sample id
state.snapshot: machine.selected_sample_id persists with serde-default current-reader backward compatibility; missing ids normalize to first available sample, while present unknown ids are rejected so stale selections cannot silently point at the wrong sample; older-binary forward compatibility is deferred

outputs: SampleSelected with SampleCatalogEntry metadata
LcdChanged after successful navigation
Ignored reason sample_catalog.empty when no assignments exist

lcd.sample: index/count, id/name, source pad, deterministic length, metadata-only/no-audio-bytes notice
lcd.trim: index/count, id/name, source pad, deterministic window metadata from the current trim model, metadata-only/no-waveform notice

desktop.controls: Prev sample and Next sample route through the SAMPLE/TRIM F1/F2 soft-key path; SAMPLE data wheel also navigates the catalog

conflict_notes: This is not real MPC2000XL or JJ-OS sample recording/import/waveform editing.
No WAV bytes, factory samples, manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: TRIM edits a metadata-only generated sample playback window

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL TRIM screen, waveform, and destructive/non-destructive sample edit behavior is not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for TRIM window editing or playback/render side effects.
JJ-OS TRIM behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this slice.
The current contract is deterministic generated-sample trim metadata only.

inputs.effect: select start or end trim field

inputs.policy: start clamps to 0..=end_frame; end clamps to start_frame..=length_frames-1

inputs.effect: captures current or recorded trim window

state.sample_trim_model: program.sample_trims contains SampleTrim entries keyed by sample_id
state.validation: selected sample id must resolve when present; trim sample ids must be unique, non-empty, known, in range, and non-inverted

outputs: SampleTrimChanged with sample_id, start_frame, end_frame, window_length_frames, and selected_field
LcdChanged after successful trim edits
Ignored for zero delta or trim boundary no-ops

lcd.trim: selected field, start frame, end frame, window length, source pad, and metadata-only/no-waveform notice

playback.intent: SamplePlaybackIntent includes start_frame, end_frame, and window_length_frames for pad strikes, MIDI note-on, and restored sequence playback
playback.recording: recorded SequenceEvent playback metadata preserves the trim window after later edits

render.summary: AudioRenderSummary includes start_frame, end_frame, and window_length_frames

desktop.status: SAMPLE/TRIM status exposes selected sample trim window; TRIM status exposes selected field

conflict_notes: No real audio import is implemented.
No waveform editing is implemented.
No destructive crop is implemented.
No MPC file-format claims are made.
No JJ-OS parity is claimed.
No WAV bytes, factory samples, manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: SONG mode edits deterministic sequence-chain metadata

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL SONG screen flow, playback, conversion, and native persistence behavior are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for SONG mode behavior.
JJ-OS SONG behavior is explicitly not accepted as reference evidence for this foundation slice.
The current contract is deterministic song-chain metadata editing only.

inputs.policy: cycle selected edit field
select previous or next step, clamped at bounds
edit selected field; stored sequence clamps to 0..=98 and displays as Seq 01..99; repeats clamp to 1..=99
insert after selected step or delete selected step

state.default_song: one step with stored sequence_index 0 displayed as Seq 01 and repeats 1
state.selected_field: step by default; step, sequence, repeats cycle with cursor left/right
state.selection: selected_song_step_index is zero-based; LCD displays one-based step/count
state.minimum_length: deleting the last remaining step is ignored
state.snapshot: top-level song object persists steps, selected_step_index, and selected_field with current-reader defaults

outputs: SongStepSelected plus LcdChanged when selection changes
SongStepChanged plus LcdChanged when sequence or repeats changes
SongStepInserted, SongStepSelected, and LcdChanged for SONG F2 insert
SongStepDeleted, SongStepSelected, and LcdChanged for successful SONG F3 delete
Ignored for zero-delta edits, step-selection bounds, and delete-last-step

lcd.title: SONG
lcd.content: selected step index/count, selected sequence number/name, repeats, selected field marker, Insert/Delete soft keys

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec metadata editor, not full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS SONG behavior.
Song playback, conversion, native file formats, and manual parity are deferred.
No proprietary manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, samples, media, binary fixtures, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

No firmware trace has been captured for SETUP mode behavior.
JJ-OS SETUP behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this internal foundation slice.
The current contract is deterministic preference editing only.

inputs.policy: cycle selected setup field
edit selected field; metronome toggles by delta sign, count-in clamps to 0..=4, LCD contrast clamps to 0..=10
unmapped

state.defaults: metronome enabled, count-in bars 0, LCD contrast 5, selected field metronome
state.selected_field: metronome, count_in_bars, lcd_contrast cycle with cursor left/right
state.snapshot: top-level setup object persists preferences and selected_field; missing top-level setup restores defaults; present incomplete setup objects are rejected

outputs: SetupPreferencesChanged plus LcdChanged when selected field changes
SetupPreferencesChanged plus LcdChanged when a preference value changes
Ignored for zero-delta edits, preference boundaries, and SETUP soft keys

lcd.title: SETUP
lcd.content: selected-field marker and current metronome, count-in bars, and LCD contrast values

desktop.status: summarizes selected SETUP field and current preferences

conflict_notes: This is an internal-spec preferences editor, not full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS SETUP behavior.
Manual, firmware, native project, real metronome audio, hardware LCD calibration, and JJ-OS parity are deferred.
No proprietary manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, samples, media, binary fixtures, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: Count-in gates recording/playhead advancement and emits deterministic click intents

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL count-in and metronome behavior is not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for count-in transport behavior.
JJ-OS count-in behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this internal foundation slice.
The current contract is deterministic count-in state and MachineOutput click intents only.

inputs.policy: 0 disables pre-roll; 1..=4 sets count-in bars
when false, count-in still gates transport but emits no MetronomeClick outputs
starts count-in when recording is armed and count_in_bars > 0
uses existing tempo-to-96-PPQN tick math to consume pre-roll
playback intent can still emit, but sequence events do not record while count-in is active
clears active count-in

state.playhead: does not advance during active count-in; normal advancement starts on the next tick batch after completion
state.recording: pad/MIDI strikes are not stored as SequenceEvent while active count-in is true
state.snapshot: active count-in fields are transient and not persisted in project snapshots

outputs: CountInStarted when REC+PLAY or OVERDUB starts a nonzero count-in
MetronomeClick for each count-in beat when metronome_enabled is true
CountInCompleted when pre-roll ticks are exhausted
No MetronomeClick outputs when metronome_enabled is false

desktop.status: summarizes count-in started, completed, and click outputs

conflict_notes: This is an internal deterministic transport foundation and not full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS count-in behavior.
No real metronome audio, audio-device routing, external sync, hardware timing tolerance, native project format, or manual parity is claimed.
No proprietary manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, samples, media, binary fixtures, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: TIMING CORRECT edits deterministic quantize and swing recording settings

source_coverage.note: Exact MPC2000XL TIMING CORRECT screens, quantize divisions, swing law, and recording behavior are not mapped yet.
No firmware trace has been captured for TIMING CORRECT behavior.
JJ-OS TIMING CORRECT behavior is not accepted as reference evidence for this internal foundation slice.
The current contract is deterministic 96 PPQN quantize/swing recording only.

inputs.policy: toggle selected field between division and swing
cycle division by delta sign or clamp swing to 50..=75
when playing and recording, record quantized tick if division is not off

state.defaults: division off, swing 50, selected field division
state.selected_field: machine selected_timing_correct_field persists current edit focus
state.snapshot: missing timing-correct fields restore defaults; present malformed settings are rejected

outputs: TimingCorrectChanged plus LcdChanged when selected field or setting changes
TimingCorrectApplied before SequenceEventRecorded when quantization changes the recorded tick
Ignored for zero-delta edits and swing boundaries

lcd.title: TIMING
lcd.content: division, swing percent, selected field marker, and internal-spec/evidence-pending line

recording.grid: 96 PPQN internal grid: eighth 48, eighth triplet 32, sixteenth 24, sixteenth triplet 16, thirty-second 12
recording.swing: odd non-triplet targets move later inside the two-step pair; triplet divisions ignore swing for now
recording.playback: restored playback schedules from the quantized recorded tick

desktop.status: TIMING mode button plus compact TC field/value controls

conflict_notes: This is an internal deterministic timing-correct foundation, not full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS TIMING CORRECT behavior.
Triplet divisions intentionally ignore swing until accepted source traces define otherwise.
Manual, firmware, hardware capture, native project, and JJ-OS parity are deferred.
No proprietary manuals, service scans, firmware bytes, JJ-OS assets, samples, media, binary fixtures, or copied third-party code are stored in the repo.

behaviors.name: SAMPLE mode creates recorded/imported sample metadata identities

source_coverage.note: Manual mapping for real SAMPLE record/import workflows is deferred; this slice is not accepted as manual parity.
No firmware trace or native project sample-import metadata comparison has been captured.
No JJ-OS parity claim is made for SAMPLE record/import behavior.
The current contract is deterministic metadata-only source identity and length propagation.

inputs.policy: create recorded metadata and assign it to the selected program pad
create imported metadata and assign it to the selected program pad

state.sample_source_kind: generated defaults remain generated; F3 creates recorded and F4 creates imported
state.length_frames: generated uses pad-derived length; recorded uses 44100; imported uses 88200
state.assignment: new metadata replaces the selected pad assignment and selects the new sample id
state.snapshot: old generated snapshots without source/length restore; malformed present recorded/imported metadata is rejected

outputs: SampleMetadataCreated before PadAssignmentChanged, SampleSelected, and LcdChanged
PadAssignmentChanged uses assigned for metadata creation
Ignored is returned for impossible metadata allocation instead of panicking

lcd.title: SAMPLE
lcd.content: source kind, sample id, source pad, effective length, and metadata-only boundary

desktop.controls: Record meta and Import meta buttons enabled in SAMPLE mode
desktop.status: created sample source kind, name, target pad, and length

conflict_notes: This is an internal deterministic metadata-only foundation, not full MPC2000XL or JJ-OS SAMPLE behavior.
No real audio bytes, file paths, binary blobs, external media, manuals, service scans, firmware, or proprietary data are stored.
